episode_summary: A bonus compilation charity episode packed with rapid‑fire curios and oddities, from NASCAR’s sticker headlights and eel houses to records for cycling backwards, bears using combs, solved checkers, and WWII bathtime strategy demos—interspersed with appeals to support the Care Workers Charity.

facts.fact: The world record for cycling backwards is 333 kilometres in a day. And this is done by an Australian guy called Helinger.
Grizzly bears can do one thing that I can't do. And that is user comb.
I have a historical use of Lufa's, which may have changed the course of the Second World War. So, a Lufa was used in the Second World War to convince Churchill of a point, okay?
